title: The Transfiguration of the Commonplace
subtitle: A Philosophy of Art
author: Arthur C. Danto
excerpt: Arthur Danto's magnum opus on the philosophy of art argues that the production of works of art that cannot be told from ordinary things makes urgent the need for a new theory. Danto develops an account of art as embodied meaning, organized around the concepts of representation, interpretation, metaphor, style, and expression.
